item.title: ‘A Mass Disaster Nonstop’: Inside the Turmoil at Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s C.D.C.
item.description: Forty-three current and former C.D.C. employees on the changes they say are replacing science with ideology — and making Americans more vulnerable.

item.title: Takeaways From The Times’s Inside Look at the C.D.C.
item.description: Many current and former employees say the actions of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. are undermining the agency’s role in safeguarding public health.

item.title: Yes, Improv Comedy Sucks. And Everyone Should Try It.
item.description: What better way to get over my decision paralysis than squaring off with the unknown for three hours a week?

item.title: The Epstein Scandal Has Reached the Far-Right Meme Stage
item.description: Once the Epstein files transitioned from an abstract concept to a real-world event, it became more difficult for fringe conspiracy theorists to control the story.

item.title: Saving Hermit Crabs by Breeding Them in the Suburbs
item.description: Often treated as throwaway pets, hermit crabs can live 50 years. Mary Akers, a self-taught expert, wants people to appreciate them as much as she does.

item.title: Every Pentagon Has Its Buzzword. For Hegseth’s, It’s ‘Lethality.’
item.description: It’s blunt instead of vague, brash instead of evasive, bold instead of cautious. And yet the word obfuscates as much as old defense jargon.

item.title: Can Seniors Order Off the Kids’ Menu?
What Is YouTube’s Dominance Doing to Us? We Asked Its C.E.O.
item.description: Neal Mohan on A.I. slop, parental controls and his platform’s impact on our lives.

item.title: Can We Ask Our Son to Go on Weight-Loss Drugs in Exchange for a House?
item.description: My husband wants to condition the gift on our son’s getting on a GLP-1 program. I feel that a gift is a gift and you should not extort a grown man.

item.title: My Friend Wrote a Book About Her Trauma. Do I Have to Read It?
item.description: She told me that anyone who won’t read her book is not her friend and that I am contributing to the greater problem in the world.

item.title: They Grew Up With Smartphones. This Is How They Live Without Them.
item.description: We asked six young New Yorkers who have taken the leap.

item.title: Is There Life After Smartphones?
item.description: What happens when a growing cohort of young people decide to ditch their devices?

item.title: Worried About A.I. Taking Your Job? That’s Not Very ‘Agentic’ of You.
item.description: Today’s spin on the idea of personal agency is convenient for tech C.E.O.s, who boast that their models work just fine without us.

item.title: The Unlikely TV Show Restoring Everyone’s Faith in Dating
item.description: Without exploitation, “Love on the Spectrum” captures the triumphs and travails of dating. It has become one of Netflix’s most popular shows.
